observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and other member of the Local Finance Committee to regularly monitor and review their collection performance efficiency on Real Property collections and other Business and other local taxes to ensure performance and high revenue generation.

We recommended that:
a. The Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC Chairman and/or BAC Secretariat to assist in the preparation and consolidation of PPMPs to Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor for audit and verification.
b. The BAC members committed to require every office to submit their proposed budget on their supplies and materials so that PPMP be prepared for their respective offices.

1.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require and supervise the Inventory Committee of the municipality to
conduct the physical count of PPE and update and submit the RPCPPE to
the Office of the Auditor; and
b. Require the General Services Office to maintain Property Cards and to
reconcile their records on a regular monthly basis with the Accounting
Office.

2.6 We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer shall not issue check to any kind of
disbursements including procurements of supplies, goods and
without the complete documents attached to the disbursement vouchers; and
b. The Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the
supporting documents required under RA 9184 and COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated July 14, 2012 on all kinds of procurement
disbursements regardless of amount before it is submitted to the
Office of the Auditor.

3.6 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly statement
of the loans account and the reports of the interest expenses charged
by the lending bank to provide accurate balance and information on
its status, and the total amount interest paid during the year in
order to arrive at the accurate balance of the liability at the end of
the year; and
b. Keep complete file on the loan documents for ready reference and
verification.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Local Finance Committee to monitor and review their
collection performance efficiency on revenue collection and
operation to ensure efficient performance and high revenue
generation.
b. The Municipal Assessor to conduct tax mapping on taxable real
properties subject for RPT to generate tax revenues; and
c. The Municipal Treasurer to enhance its collection techniques and
procedures.

5.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC
Chairman and/or BAC Secretariat to assist in the preparation and
consolidation of PPMPs to Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and furnish
a copy to the Office of the Auditor for audit and verification.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and/or the Municipal
Accountant shall attached the required documents provided under
Section 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 upon
submission of the payrolls of the salaries and wages of laborers/workers
for maintenance services to justify the regularity and validity of the
transactions.

7.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) for every transaction and be attached to each
Disbursement Vouchers or Payroll, collections and deposits or even non-
cash transactions for appropriate recording to the books of accounts.
Correct and accurate journal entry result to a fair and reliable financial
statements.

8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoined that:
a. The Municipal Treasurer submit the monthly RCI, ROD, RCD and
other reports attached with the complete supporting papers to the
Municipal Accountant within the 5th day of the ensuing month;
b. The Municipal Accountant record and post the financial
transactions to the books of account then submit to the Auditor
within the time frame of submission prescribed by law.
c. There has to be an appoint/designate additional support personnel
to the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office to expedite the
preparation of financial reports and recording to the books of
accounts; and
d. The Municipal Treasurer devise an effective system to shorten the
processing of payment of claims and disbursements to facilitate the
timely preparation of RCI’s and ROD’s.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enforce that:
a) No granting of additional cash advance to any accountable officer
unless the previous cash advance is first settled; and
b) The accountable Officer shall report and liquidate his cash
advance as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been
served; and
c) The accountable officer submit the payroll for the unliquidated
cash advances for the period January to March, 2022.

10.6 We recommended that the Management:
a. Submit copies of the contracts of agreement duly notarized of all
Contract of Service employees within five (5) working days after its
execution to the Office of the Auditor for verification of its
authenticity; and
b. Furnish a copy of the contract first to the Audit Team before
releasing the first salaries of these COS as prerequisite in the
payment of first salary of employees to safeguard and prevent
mishandling of government funds.

11.5 We recommended that Management submit the documents required
under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other existing
laws, rules and regulations to settle our audit suspensions pursuant to
Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that :
a. Municipal Accountant to see to it that complete supporting documents as required under RA 9184 and re-iterated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 on all kinds of procurement regardless of amount be submitted to the Office of the Auditor; and
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to observed faithfully the guidelines on the procurements of goods and materials.
c. All other municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in all stages of the procurement process.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Accountant ensure attachment of Accomplishment Reports of each casual, contractual and/or job order personnel to establish the validity and legality of the payments of their salaries and wages for the certain period of time.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Mayor enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to authorized the management to obligate and disburse the funds appropriated for the financial assistance granted to the indigenous constituents of the municipality as required under Section 396 of GAAM Volume 1.
b. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that the documentary evidence for the payments of cash assistance were complete before payments were made thereof.

We recommended that Management strictly adheres with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in granting cash advances; and ensure that cash advances were utilized for the purpose for it was granted.

We recommended that Management submit the documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other existing laws, rules and regulations to settle our audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1.We recommended that Management require the accountant to compute, recognize and record in the books of accounts the depreciation expenses of the affected PPE accounts by drawing a journal entry voucher and update/post the Journals and Ledger.

14.We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to observe and practice the stamping of “PAID” stamped to every Disbursement Vouchers and all its supporting documents after payments were made and indicate therein the number of check issued and the date of the actual payment.

1.4 We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of their Road
Networks and Water Supply Systems to reflect its correct carrying
amounts as of year then ended.

2.6 We recommended that Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Treasurer
and other member of the Local Finance Committee to regularly monitor
and review their collection performance efficiency on Real Property
collections and other Business and other local taxes to ensure
performance and high revenue generation.

3.6 We recommended that Local Chief Executive enjoin the BAC Chairman
and/or BAC Secretariat to assist in the preparation and consolidation of
PPMPs to APP and furnish a copy to the Office of the Auditor for audit
and verification.

4.4 We recommended that Municipal Accountant ensure attachment of
Accomplishment Reports of each casual, contractual and/or job order
personnel to establish the validity and legality of the payments of their
salaries and wages for the certain period of time.

5.4 We recommended that :
a. Municipal Mayor enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to authorize the
management to obligate and disburse the funds appropriated for the
financial assistance granted to the indigenous constituents of the
municipality as required under Section 396 of GAAM Volume 1;
and
b. The Municipal Accountant ensure that the documentary evidence for the
payments of cash assistance were complete before payments were made
thereof.

6.3 We recommended that management faithfully adheres with COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in granting cash advances
and ensure that cash advances were utilized for the purpose for it was
granted.

7.5 We recommended that Honorable Mayor enjoin the:
a. Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting
documents as required under RA 9184 and re-iterated under COA
Circular No. 2012-001 on all kinds of procurement regardless of
amount be submitted to the Office of the Auditor;
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to observe faithfully the
guidelines on the procurements of goods and materials; and
c. All other municipal officials concerned to faithfully adhere to the
provisions of the RA 9184 and its Revised IRR in all stages of the
procurement process.

8.4 We recommended that Management submit the documents required
under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other existing
laws, rules and regulations to settle our audit suspensions pursuant to
Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

2.The Municipal Accountant attached the computation of the Terminal Leave of the retiree for validation:
3.Management strictly adhere to the above cited COA Circular and other accounting rules and regulations; and
4.Direct the claimants to submit the above requirements to the COA Audit Team for audit, verification and analysis and other audit actions.

5.Submit Supplemental Budget and Supplemental Investment Program with the approved Sangguniang Bayan
6.The Municipal Accountant to properly record and prepare separate account for the BAYANIHAN GRANTS in accordance with the generally accepted accounting rules and regulations; and
7.Municipal Treasurer must stamped “PAID” all Disbursement Vouchers and all its supporting documents pertaining to the BAYANIHAN GRANTS to avoid repetition of payments of expenses.

8.Refrain from granting cash advances without first liquidating the previous cash advance; and
9.Ensure that the documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001 be properly complied.

13.We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Reports of Collections and Deposits for the calendar year 2021 for the funds received and collected together with the duplicate copy of the official receipt and deposits to validate the total collections of revenues presented in the financial statements for the end of the year.

15.We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to post a tarpaulin signboard which must be suitably framed outdoor display at the project location or its activities with all the information as provided in Annex A pursuant to paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

16.We recommended that Management submit the documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and other existing laws, rules and regulations to comply with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

17.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly statement of accounts on the loans and establish the accurate balance and information on the total amortization and interest paid during the year Keep complete file on the loan documents for ready reference and verification.

18.Strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
19.Submit to this office the following documents: Local Disaster Risk Reduction Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

20.We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned agency officials to observe the fundamental principles provided under Section 4 of PD 1445 and other pertinent regulations under COA Circular No. 2012 dated July 4, 2012 on the disbursements pertaining to the training and seminar expenses of the agency.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly statement of accounts on the loans and establish the accurate balance and information on the total amortization and interest paid during the year in order to arrive at the accurate balance of the liability at the end of the year. Keep complete file on the loan documents for ready reference and verification.

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014; and
b. Submit to this office the following documents: Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned agency officials to observe the fundamental principles provided under Section 4 of PD 1445 and other pertinent regulations under COA Circular No. 2012 dated July 4, 2012 on the disbursements pertaining to the training and seminar expenses of the agency.

We recommended that Management require the accountant to compute, recognize and record in the books of accounts the depreciation expenses of the affected PPE accounts by drawing a journal entry voucher and update/post the same with its corresponding Journals and Ledgers.

a. The Municipal Accountant attached the computation of the
Terminal Leave of the retiree for validation; and
b. Management strictly adhere to the above cited COA Circular and
other accounting rules and regulations; and
c. Direct the claimants to submit the above requirements to the COA
Audit Team for audit, verification and analysis and other audit
actions.

We recommended that Management submit the following required
documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification and audit, viz:
a. Supplemental Budget and the Supplemental Annual Investment
Plan supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution/Ordinance;
and
b. Lastly, submit the list/names of beneficiaries of relief goods, if any,
to support proof of distribution of welfare goods expenses.

a. Refrain from granting cash advances without first liquidating the
previous cash advance; and
b. Ensure that the documentary requirements prescribed under
Circular 2012-001 be properly complied with.

Hence, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Reports
of Collections and Deposits for the calendar year 2020 together with the
copies of the official receipt and validated deposit slips to support the
total collections of revenues presented in the financial statements for the
end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe and practice
the stamping of "PAID" stamped to every Disbursement Vouchers and
all its supporting documents after payments were made and indicate
therein the number of check issued and the date of the actual payment.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to
post a tarpaulin signboard which must be suitably framed outdoor
display at the project location or its activities with all the information as
provided in Annex A (attached for ready reference) pursuant to
paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require/instruct the
responsible/liable persons to submit the required documents or lacking
supporting papers within 90 days from receipt of an NS, otherwise, such
suspensions will become disallowance and settlement thereof will be
immediately enforced upon issuance of the Certificate of Execution by
withholding the salaries or other claims due the persons liable in
satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal person to facilitate the formulation of GAD Plans and Budget (GPB) and ensure that activities identified as GAD issues and concerns are properly addressed pursuant to the provisions under PCW-DILG-DBM- NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 and require management to submit the Accomplishment Report on GAD Activities as required under the aforementioned regulations.

We recommended that Management:
a.Create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment.
b.Prepare annual Reports on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
c.Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.
d. After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment which should bear a Property Number.

We recommended that Management:
5. strictly follow the rules and regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, and
6. submit to this office the following documents:
a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP)
b. Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

7. We recommended that Management create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment.
8. Prepare annual Reports on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
9. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.
10. After physical count and report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment which should bear a Property Number.
11. The Municipal accountant shall provide residual value in computing the depreciation of each asset. Obsolete and fully depreciated property account shall be transferred to Other Assets account.

12. We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal person to facilitate the formulation of GAD Plans and Budget (GPB) and ensure that activities identified as issues and concerns are properly addressed pursuant to the provisions under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 and require management to submit the Accomplishment Report on GAD Activities as required.

1.6 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer and/or the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly
statement of accounts on the loans and establish the accurate balance
and information on the total amortization and interest paid at the end of
the year. We further recommended that only the portion of the Loans
which are due and demandable in the next calendar year should be
presented in the Current Liabilities while the remaining balance should
be in the Non-Current Liabilities of the Statement of Financial Position.

3.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the concerned
agency officials to observe the fundamental principles provided under
Section 4 of PD 1445 and other pertinent regulations under COA
Circular No. 2012 dated July 4, 2012 on the disbursements pertaining to
the training and seminar expenses of the agency.

5.5 We recommended that Honorable Mayor:

a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended
LDRRM Fund which represents the Quick Response Fund (QRF)
from General Fund to Special Trust Fund.

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of
unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher
taking the appropriate accounting entries under General Fund
and Trust Fund.

i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of
Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds
(Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash
in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code
1-01-02-010).

ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in
Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-
01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities –
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account
Code 2-04-01-020).

6.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor require the municipal
employees responsible/liable for the suspended/disallowed
transactions to submit the required documents within 90 days from
receipt thereof. Otherwise, it will be disallowed in audit and settlement
thereof should be immediately enforced by withholding the salaries or
other claims due the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts
disallowed which is final and executory.

7.4 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:

a) Direct the concerned municipal official to reconcile the agency
records with GSIS Iligan Service Office to establish the actual
arrearages of the municipality with the GSIS and settle
arrearages of the unremitted life and retirement insurance
contributions of the municipal employees, and

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize the unremitted
contributions to establish the accurate and reliable balance of
the inter agency payables as presented in the financial
statements at the end of the period.

We recommended that Management:
1. submit Contract of Service and Job Descriptions/Specifications of Contractual and Casual employees and observe the following measures:
a. Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses by the municipal government, and
b. Attached to the Daily Wage Payroll copy of the Contract of Service stipulated therein the specific purpose, work assignment and the specific duties and responsibilities of the job order employees.

We recommended that Management instruct the Office of the Accountant to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) of its Depository Bank Account with the Philippine National Bank, Marawi Branch, Marawi City and submit it monthly together with the supporting documents such as original copies of bank statements, debit/credit memos (DM/CM) and paid checks to the Office of the Auditor as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officer to record promptly all checks issued in the cashbook so that it can be properly accounted for easy reconciliation of the same could be made.
4. We further recommended that Management initiate to improve the system of recording transactions with the use of checks issued in numerical sequence or in chronological order as reference pursuant to Section 41 of NGAs Manual, Volume I.

13. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to record promptly all collections in the cashbook-Cash in Treasury and the Municipal Accountant also record the RPT collection to its Subsidiary Ledger pursuant to Section 13 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I for proper accountability, monitoring and verification.
14. We also recommended that Management improve the system of recording of income and revenues to the agency’s books of accounts and its prompt deposits to the agency authorized depository bank and issuance of official receipts must be in numerical sequence or in chronological order in compliance with accounting system for collections.

19. We recommended to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) that R.A. 9184 and its IRR shall be religiously observed and followed in the implementation of infrastructure projects and shall not approve payments to Human Safety Site Consultancy of the projects cost unless complete documents have been submitted to the agency pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.
20. We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to submit immediately bid documents and its supporting documents for audit and evaluation, considering the period lapsed in the submission of reports to the Auditor.

21. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the annual SCBAA for the year ended December 31, 2018 to complete the submission of the financial statements at year end pursuant to IPSASs.

22. We recommended that the local Chief Executive shall compel all regular Municipal officials and employees appointed or elected including contractual employees to file or update their membership in the GSIS, PHILHEALTH and PAG-IBIG offices and deduct from their monthly their monthly contributions and remit with the respective GOCC’s in compliance to GSIS Act and R.A. 7742.

1.4. We recommended that Management submit the Contract of Service and
Job Descriptions/Specifications of contractual or casual employees and
observe the following measures:

a. Consider and limit the hiring of job orders to extremely necessary
work assignment to prevent the incurrence of unnecessary expenses
by the municipal government;
b. Attached to the Daily Wage Payroll copy of the Contract of Service
stipulated therein the specific purpose, work assignment and the
specific duties and responsibilities of the job order employees.

2.5. We recommended that Management instruct the Office of the Accountant
to prepare Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) of its Depository Bank
Account with the Philippine National Bank, Marawi Branch, Marawi
City and submit it monthly together with the supporting documents such
as original copies of bank statements, debit/credit memos (DM/CM) and
paid checks to the Office of the Auditor as prescribed by COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

3.6. We recommended that Management require the Accountable Officer to
record promptly all checks issued in the cashbook so that it can be
properly accounted for easy reconciliation of the same could be made.

3.7. We further recommended that Management initiate to improve the
system of recording transactions with the use of checks issued in
numerical sequence or in chronological order as reference pursuant to
Section 41 of NGAs Manual, Volume I.

4.4. We recommended that Management strictly follow the rules and
regulations set forth by COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,
2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and submit to
this office the following documents:

a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP), and the
b. Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis

5.10. We recommended that Management create an appraisal/inventory
committee to conduct actual physical inventory taking of all properties,
plant and equipment and prepare annual Reports on Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Also direct the Municipal
Accountant to make necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies
noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the
inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers. After physical count and
report of PPE’s are completed, prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger
card on each equipment which should bear a Property Number. The
municipal accountant shall provide residual value in computing the
30

depreciation of each asset. Obsolete and fully depreciated property
account shall be transferred to Other Assets account.

6.5. We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal person to
facilitate the formulation of GAD Plans and Budget (GPB) and ensure
that activities identified as GAD issues and concerns are properly
addressed pursuant to the provisions under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular 2014-001
dated March 18, 2014 and require management to submit the
Accomplishment Report on GAD Activities as required under the
aforementioned regulations.

7.5. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to
record promptly all collections in the cashbook-Cash in Treasury and the
Municipal Accountant also record the RPT collection to its Subsidiary
Ledger pursuant to Section 13 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I for
proper accountability, monitoring and verification.

7.6. We also recommended that Management improve the system of recording
of income and revenues to the agency’s books of accounts and its prompt
deposits to the agency authorized depository bank and issuance of official
receipts must be in numerical sequence or in chronological order in
compliance with accounting system for collections.

8.7. We recommended that Management comply with the requirements of the
provisions of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184. RA 9184 further provides that
“in the preparation of the indicative APP, the end-user or implementing
units of the Procuring Entity shall formulate their respective PPMP for
their different PAPs. PPMP shall include:

a. Information on whether PAPs will be contracted out, implemented by
administration or consigned;
b. The type and objective of contract to be employed;
c. The extent/size of contract scopes/packages.
d. The procurement methods to be adopted, and indicating if the
procurement tasks are to be outsourced as provided in Sec of the
IRR;
e. The time schedule for each procurement activity and for the contract
implementation; and
f. The estimated budget for the general components

8.8. The PPMPs included in the budget proposal shall be forwarded to the
BAC Secretariat for consolidation into an indicative APP and to the
BAC for final recommendation of the appropriate procurement
modality.

8.9. Changes to the individual PPMPs and the consolidated APP may be
undertaken every 6 months or as often as may be required by the HOPE.
The respective end-user or implementing units of the PE shall be
responsible for the changes to the PPMPs, while the BAC Secretariat
shall be responsible for the consolidation of these PPMPs into the APP,
subject to approval by the HOPE.

8.10. We also recommended that Management prepare PPMP at the beginning
of the year in support of the APP, in order to have a well-planned and
coordinated procurement, thus achieving economy and efficiency in
government transactions.

9.6. We recommended to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) that R.A.
9184 and its IRR shall be religiously observed and followed in the
implementation of infrastructure projects and shall not approve
payments to Human Safety Site Consultancy of the projects cost unless
complete documents have been submitted to the agency pursuant to
Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.

9.7. We further recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant and other concerned officials to submit immediately bid
documents and its supporting documents for audit and evaluation,
considering the period lapsed in the submission of reports to the Auditor.

11.2. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and submit the annual SCBAA for the year ended December 31,
2018 to complete the submission of the financial statements at year end
pursuant to IPSASs.

12.7 We recommended that the local Chief Executive shall compel all regular
municipal officials and employees appointed or elected including
contractual employees to file or update their membership in the
GSIS, PHILHEALTH and PAG-IBIG offices and deduct from their
monthly their monthly contributions and remit with the respective
GOCC’s in compliance to GSIS Act and R.A. 7742.

We recommend that Management  require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare Reports of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and submit to the Municipal Accountant for the latter to update her records and arrived with the actual cash balance at the end of the period. Municipal Treasurer was also required to reconcile his cash accounts balances with the Municipal Accountant’s records at least every quarter and verify if there are discrepancies in their records and effect immediately any adjustments and corrections.

We recommend that the Local Executive create a municipal inventory team to conduct at least once every end of the year a physical count of the LGU’s PPE and prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) to the concerned audit team.
We also recommend that the Accounting Office and the Supply Unit  record and post all procurements and projects completed to the property records or ledgers to  facilitate inventory or physical of count of  the LGU’s property, plant and equipment.

We recommend that Management direct the concerned officials to submit the required documents enumerated above to support the legality of the check payments made in favor of the Philippines Charity Sweepstakes amounting to P 343,200.00 as counter-part of the municipality on the cost of the one unit (1) ambulance vehicle released to the municipal government of Mulondo, Lanao del Sur.
Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to record in the agency’s books of accounts the total cost of one (1) unit motor vehicle  in order not to understate the total assets and net equity accounts of the municipality at the end of the year. Make the necessary adjustments and corrections on these accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer shall not release the payments of the financial assistance to the recipients without SB Resolution authorizing the payments thereof and assured that all documentary evidence for the claims is complete before payment is made.

We recommend that the Local Executive shall require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer(MDRRMO) to coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Accountant and furnish her a report on the actual programs, projects, activities and expenditures pertaining to the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund so that the latter can submit on 	time 	the monthly Reports on the Source and Utilization of LDRRMF pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Require the Management to observe the diligence of a good father of a family during emergency purchase of supplies for the repair and maintenance of motor vehicle.  Sections 87 to 90 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 shall be diligently observed to avoid sanctions of laws under RA 7160. Request from the concern official for the pre and post inspection report during the repair.

We recommend that Management establish or create the GAD Focal Point in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2004-1 of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW who will advocates for, coordinates, guides and monitors the development and implementation of the agency’s GAD plan and GAD-related programs, activities and projects.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the disallowances in the books of accounts as  Receivables-Disallowance/Charges (1-03006-010) and become collectible from the persons liable of the disallowances. Reflect the accounts in the financial statements as of the end of the year. The amount returned/refunded could be an additional fund of the municipality to finance its operations for the interest of public service.

Create focal Point System of the Municipality of Mulondo composed 	of heads of the different departments of the agency to facilitate the 	institutionalization of Gender Mainstreaming, set the GAD agenda for the 3-	year period and prioritized the gender issues to be addressed by the 	municipal government. They will also formulate the GAD plans, programs, 	projects and activities to be integrated in the GAD Plan and Budget in order 	to be complaint of the rules and regulations provided in the JC 2004-1 of 	DBM-NCRFW and Republic Act 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women

We recommend that the Municipal  Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer shall prepare and formulate development plans and projects  aligned  with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM to include in the deliberation of the Sanggunian Bayan during the preparation of the budget and to appropriate funds out of the 20% economic development funds of the Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipal government.
We recommend that management submit copy of the Contract of Service of contractual/ casual employees to evaluate the job description and job specification of each approved job order.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account (5-02-99-990) which are applicable for expenditures related to the advertisements of selling of products and services and the publication of invitation to bid for procurement of goods and services and contract as required under the  PPSAS Revised Chart of Accounts.

Create Local Solid Waste Management Board to formulate and prepare plans and programs on the solid waste management of the municipality to promote public health and sanitation and promote environment awareness in compliance to Sections 12 and 16 of R.A. 9003.

Create focal Point System of the Municipality of Mulondo composed of heads of the different departments of the agency to facilitate the institutionalization of Gender Mainstreaming, set the GAD agenda for the 3-year period and prioritized the gender issues to be addressed by the municipal government. They will also formulate the GAD plans, programs, projects and activities to be integrated in the GAD Plan and Budget in order to be complaint of the rules and regulations provided in the JC 2004-1 of DBM-NCRFW and Republic Act 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the disallowances in the books of accounts as  Receivables-Disallowance/Charges (1-03006-010) and become collectible from the persons liable of the disallowances. Reflect the accounts in the financial statements as of the end of the year. Enforce the immediate settlement of the audit disallowances or deduct from the salary of the concerned or persons liable. The amount returned/refunded could be an additional fund of the municipality to finance its operations for the interest of public service.

We recommend that the Municipal  Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer shall prepare and formulate development plans and projects  aligned  with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM to include in the deliberation of the Sanggunian Bayan during the preparation of the budget and to appropriate funds out of the 20% economic development funds of the Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipal government.
We recommend to the management submit copy of the Contract of Service of contractual/ casual employees to evaluate the job description and job specification of each approved job order.
